title: Virological characteristics of the SARS-CoV-2 XBB variant derived from recombination of two Omicron subvariants
classification_notes: GPT error: Expecting property name enclosed in double quotes: line 184 column 152 (char 11477)

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies the SARS-CoV-2 Omicron recombinant lineage XBB/XBB.1, combining phylogenetics, neutralization assays, ACE2-binding measurements, pseudovirus and cell-fusion assays, cryo-EM structures, cell-culture growth kinetics, and hamster infection experiments. The main finding is that XBB likely arose by recombination between BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1, gained strong immune evasion and increased fusogenicity/fitness, but did not show higher intrinsic pathogenicity than BA.2.75 in hamsters.
measurement_types: epidemic frequency / effective reproduction number estimation
viral growth kinetics in cell culture
hamster infection / body weight / pulmonary function

figures.description: Maximum-likelihood phylogenetic tree of representative Omicron lineages, showing XBB parental clades BJ.1 and BM.1.1.1.
Amino-acid differences in spike proteins across Omicron lineages.
Recombination breakpoint analysis and nucleotide-difference visualization between BJ.1, BM.1, and XBB consensus sequences.
Time-calibrated phylogeny of the nonrecombinant 5' segment and ML phylogeny of the 3' segment, used to infer XBB emergence timing.
Estimated relative effective reproduction numbers (Re) for XBB-related lineages in India.
Geographic distribution / lineage frequency map comparing BQ.1 and XBB prevalence by country.
Hierarchical Bayesian estimates of relative Re values for BQ.1 and XBB lineages.
Estimated lineage dynamics over time in countries where BQ.1 and XBB cocirculated.
Neutralization of BA.2 breakthrough sera against XBB.1 and BA.2-derived spike mutants.
Neutralization of BA.5 breakthrough sera against XBB.1 and BA.2-derived spike mutants.
Neutralization by 4-dose monovalent vaccine sera.
Neutralization by BA.1 bivalent vaccine sera.
Neutralization by BA.5 bivalent vaccine sera.
Neutralization of hamster sera from infections with BA.2, BA.5, BQ.1.1, BA.2.75, and XBB.1.
Antigenic cartography based on hamster neutralization data.
Yeast surface display measurement of RBD-ACE2 binding affinity (KD) for BA.2, XBB.1, and BA.2-derived mutants.
Pseudovirus infectivity in HOS-ACE2/TMPRSS2 cells for BA.2, XBB.1, and mutants.
Cell-surface expression level of spike proteins in the fusion assay system.
S protein-mediated cell-cell fusion kinetics in Calu-3 cells for BA.2, BA.2.75, XBB.1, and mutants.
Cryo-EM maps and structural comparison of XBB.1 spike closed-1 and closed-2 conformations.
Close-up structural differences between closed-1 and closed-2 states, including RBD-HR1 and fusion peptide regions.
Cryo-EM maps of XBB.1 spike bound to ACE2 in one-up, two-up, and local RBD-ACE2 refined states.
Atomic model of the XBB.1 RBD-ACE2 interface, including comparison to BA.2.75 and ACE2 glycan interactions.
Growth kinetics of clinical isolates in Vero cells.
Growth kinetics in Calu-3 cells.
Growth kinetics in human airway organoid-derived air-liquid interface cultures.
Growth kinetics in human iPSC-derived airway epithelial cells.
Growth kinetics in VeroE6/TMPRSS2 cells.
Growth kinetics in human iPSC-derived lung epithelial cells.
Airway-on-a-chip setup showing top and bottom channels used to assess viral invasion across the barrier.
Percentage of virus invading from the top to bottom channel in the airway-on-a-chip system.
Hamster body weight and pulmonary function metrics (Penh, Rpef) after infection with Delta, BA.2.75, XBB.1, or saline.
Viral RNA loads in oral swabs and lung regions of infected hamsters.
Immunohistochemistry for viral N protein in hamster lungs and quantification of N-positive cells.
Representative H&E-stained lung pathology images from infected and uninfected hamsters.
Histopathological scoring of lung lesions across infection groups.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Yes. The paper reports raw experimental measurements from neutralization assays (NT50 values from triplicate assays), yeast surface display binding measurements (KD), pseudovirus infectivity, fusion assay readouts, cryo-EM data, RT-qPCR viral RNA loads, infectious viral titers, hamster body weight and pulmonary function, immunohistochemistry counts, and histopathology scores. It also uses sequence data retrieved from GISAID for phylogenetic/recombination analyses and epidemic inference.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that source data are provided with the paper for the figures shown. It also uses SARS-CoV-2 sequences deposited to GISAID for phylogenetic and recombination analyses. The excerpt does not mention a separate public repository for raw experimental files or analysis scripts.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are not described in detail in the excerpt as downloadable files or instrument files. The paper indicates collection of experimental measurements from pseudovirus assays, yeast display, cryo-EM, cell culture, hamster infection, RT-qPCR, IHC, and histology, but does not specify raw file formats or a raw-data repository in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include figure-ready values such as NT50 neutralization titers, KD values, infectivity percentages, fusion activity, viral RNA copy numbers, posterior Re estimates, lineage frequencies, antigenic cartography coordinates, and histopathology scores. The paper also uses consensus sequences and aligned sequence sets for recombination and phylogenetic inference.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Yes. Multiple figure captions explicitly state 'Source data are provided with this paper.' This strongly indicates figure-level source data tables are available for the main figures, likely containing the plotted numerical values.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts or notebooks are mentioned in the provided text. Computational analyses are described methodologically (e.g., RDP5, Bayesian phylogenetics, hierarchical Bayesian model), but script availability is not stated.
dataset_characterization.notes: This is a mixed dataset paper: it combines experimental virology data with computational phylogenetics/epidemiology and structural biology. The most clearly documented data-sharing element is figure source data; raw instrument-level data and code are not explicitly described in the excerpt. Sequence data are external/public via GISAID rather than newly generated and deposited in the paper text shown.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct measurements from assays and animal experiments, including NT50 neutralization titers, KD binding values, pseudovirus infectivity, fusion activity, RT-qPCR viral loads, cryo-EM structures, and histology scoring.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figure captions repeatedly state 'Source data are provided with this paper.'
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “Source data are provided with this paper.”; “We retrieved all SARS-CoV-2 sequences deposited to GISAID”; “The structures ... were determined by cryo-EM analysis”; “Assays were performed in triplicate/quadruplicate”; “Statistically significant differences ... were determined by ...”
